t: Identification of psychological factors — depression (PHQ-9), anxiety (GAD-7), medication adherence (Morisky).
Behavioral factors — smoking, alcohol, diet, physical activity.
Affective symptoms meet independent criteria — separate diagnosis.
Illness-specific scales (DDS — Diabetes Distress Scale, Beck-cardiac).
An integrated multidisciplinary approach — somatic specialist + psychiatrist + psychologist.
Motivational interviewing — behavior change (smoking, physical activity).
Levenson J.L. APA Textbook of Psychosomatic Medicine 2018.
CBT and ACT for chronic pain
Integrative approach improves both somatic and psychological outcomes.
Myth 1: "The illness is entirely psychological in origin"
"Biopsychosocial" model — psychological factors influence but do not alone cause somatic illness.
Myth 2: "The psychiatrist is 'running away' from the illness"
integrated approach — somatic and psychiatric intervention parallel.
Myth 3: "Alternative medicine (yoga, mindfulness) cures somatic illness"
Complementary interventions may be adjunctive; does not replace standard somatic treatment (oncologic, cardiac).
Myth 4: "Telling the patient 'you got sick because of stress' is therapeutic"
This approach undermines accusatory and therapeutic relationship.
WHO. ICD-11. 6E40 Psychological or behavioural factors affecting disorders or diseases classified elsewhere. 2024.
Levenson J.L. (ed.). APA Publishing Textbook of Psychosomatic Medicine. 3rd ed. 2018.
Whooley M.A., Wong J.M. Depression and cardiovascular disorders. Annu Rev Clin Psychol 2013;9:327–354.
